text: fat is 3.6g. cholesterol adds to the tradeoff. for meats, fat quality matters a lot once the protein is already there.
carbs is 3.9g. outside of that, the profile is pretty quiet. in this category, carbs barely matter.
protein is 20.4g. protein quality is a real strength here. strong protein is expected here, so the rest of the profile decides how high it climbs.
vitamin b12 at 2500% DV, vitamin d at 7% DV stand out most. that is major vitamin support.
iron at 36% DV, zinc at 37% DV, potassium at 7% DV stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

on the plus side. extreme micronutrient density because a direct stress test for whether meats underweight nutrient bombs. heme iron case is very strong because hard to ignore in meat scoring. protein quality is excellent because still a strong meat on the protein axis too.

the drawbacks next. cholesterol is extremely high because major drawback. overconsumption risk is real because extremely nutrient-dense is not automatically simple. taste is polarising because practical downside.
A tier. Best when you want efficient protein and can accept the category tradeoffs that come with it.
